prerequisites: A Microsoft 365 / SharePoint Online tenant with the SharePoint site(s) you want to read, and an account that can sign in to that tenant. Record your Directory (tenant) ID, used for the credentials.tenant_id field.

An application registered in Microsoft Entra ID (the Microsoft identity platform). Registering the app yields an Application (client) ID, used for the credentials.client_id field. Registering requires at least the Cloud Application Administrator role (or Application Developer).
A client secret created on the app registration, used for the credentials.client_secret field. The secret value is shown only once at creation and cannot be retrieved later.
Microsoft Graph delegated permissions consented for the app so it can read SharePoint content, for example Sites.Read.All and/or Files.Read.All, plus offline_access so a refresh token is issued.
The user principal name (UPN, for example user@contoso.com) of the account whose delegated access the connector uses, for the credentials.user_principal_name field.

For the streams config field, the file format streams you want to sync. Optionally the site_url of the target SharePoint site, a folder_path to scope reading, a search_scope, a file_contains_query filter, a delivery_method, and a start_date to limit by file modification time.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft identity platform, authorization code flow)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates to Microsoft Graph on behalf of a user using the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. It maps to the descriptor secret fields credentials.client_id (Application (client) ID), credentials.client_secret (the app registration client secret), credentials.tenant_id (Directory (tenant) ID), credentials.refresh_token (a long-lived refresh token, issued only when the offline_access scope is requested), and credentials.user_principal_name (the signing-in user's UPN). The refresh token is exchanged at the Microsoft identity platform /token endpoint for short-lived access tokens used to call Graph.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (entra.microsoft.com), switch to the tenant that hosts your SharePoint site, and go to Identity > Applications > App registrations > New registration. Give the app a name and choose the supported account types (single tenant is typical). After registering, open the Overview page and record the Application (client) ID (credentials.client_id) and the Directory (tenant) ID (credentials.tenant_id).

setupSteps.title: Add a client secret
setupSteps.body: On the app registration, go to Certificates & secrets > Client secrets > New client secret. Add a description and an expiration (24 months or less), then select Add. Immediately copy the secret's Value and store it as credentials.client_secret. This value is never displayed again after you leave the page.

setupSteps.title: Grant Microsoft Graph permissions for SharePoint
setupSteps.body: On the app registration, go to API permissions > Add a permission > Microsoft Graph and add the delegated permissions needed to read SharePoint content, such as Sites.Read.All and/or Files.Read.All, plus offline_access so a refresh token can be issued. Have an administrator grant admin consent for the tenant if required.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow against the Microsoft identity platform: redirect the user to the /authorize endpoint with your client_id, redirect_uri, and a scope that includes offline_access and the SharePoint scopes, then exchange the returned code at the /token endpoint (with client_secret) for tokens. The response includes a refresh_token (returned only because offline_access was requested). Store it as credentials.refresh_token, and set credentials.user_principal_name to the UPN of the account that signed in.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the credentials object (client_id, client_secret, tenant_id, refresh_token, user_principal_name) and set the required streams field to the file streams you want to sync. Optionally set site_url to target a specific SharePoint site, folder_path to scope which folder is read, search_scope, file_contains_query to filter files, delivery_method, and start_date to limit files by modification date.
